mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate on future earnings and eases financial conditions, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month when the cut is part of an active easing cycle.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal produces negative average returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound, real rates fall and investors reprice nominal assets upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform unconditional returns, or if triggered episodes show negative average 20-day SPX returns across a full 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor income and weaker consumer demand, which lowers expected corporate revenue and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of episodes after the unemployment trigger, or if the signal is not negative in at least 2 of 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS paired with a stable or falling UNRATE indicates broad job creation without hidden labor distress, supporting household income and spending and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising PAYEMS and non-rising UNRATE do not produce positive 60-day forward returns on average, or if the interaction adds no explanatory power versus PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly sales growth in RSAFS reflects resilient household and business demand, which supports revenue expectations and pushes the S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth turns positive and exceeds the prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth episodes do not beat baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect is zero/negative in a rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST after a prior surge signals cooling housing demand and weaker construction-sensitive spending, which foreshadows lower equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the housing contraction trigger is followed by positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is indistinguishable from random across 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with improving PAYEMS indicates expanding real output without labor retrenchment, a classic growth-confirmation signal that should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in industrial production and payrolls does not yield positive 60-day forward returns, or if the interaction term is not robust in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth slows while UNRATE rises, the economy is entering a demand-destruction regime that compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below 1% and UNRATE rises over the same quarter
failure_condition: Reject if the GDP-slowdown-plus-unemployment-rise condition fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or if positive returns dominate triggered episodes over any 3-year rolling window.
